description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated human figure with stylized, angular features and a shoulder-length, straight hairstyle reminiscent of ancient Egyptian art. The figure sits cross-legged on a rectangular plinth, with both hands resting on its knees, and the body posture is upright and formal. The surface of the sculpture is a mottled gray stone, with subtle variations in tone and a slightly weathered appearance. The plinth features what appear to be incised markings or inscriptions along the front edge, adding to the artifact-like quality. The overall form is solid and compact, with no visible openings or separable parts. The sculpture is displayed on a flat base with measurement and color calibration tools, suggesting it is being documented or studied.
description: A gray stone sculpture of a seated human figure with stylized features and incised markings on the plinth, reminiscent of ancient Egyptian art.
description_view_0: Side view showing the figure's profile, straight hair, and the cross-legged posture on a rectangular plinth.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the symmetrical face, both hands on knees, and visible incised markings on the front of the plinth.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, mirroring the first side, with clear view of the figure's posture and the plinth's thickness.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the head with detailed hair texture and the broad, solid back of the figure and plinth.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a flat surface with measurement and color calibration tools, suggesting an indoor documentation or study setting.
